_description: Intentional Python enhancements over JS. These are NOT bugs; they are documented Python-only features.

enhancements.description: Python accepts 13 short aliases (e.g., 'metric' -> 'metric_signature'). JS requires full field names.
enhancements.rationale: CLI ergonomics: physicists can type 'gpd convention set metric mostly-plus' instead of full field name.

enhancements.description: Python normalizes variant notations (e.g., '+---' -> 'mostly-minus'). JS stores raw values.
enhancements.rationale: Prevents convention drift from notation variants that represent the same physics.

enhancements.description: Python parses ASSERT_CONVENTION directives from Markdown/LaTeX/Python files. JS has no equivalent.
enhancements.rationale: Enables automated verification that code/documents use the locked conventions.
